attacking_style: Long-range attacks from breakaways on extended climbs (17km observed), exploiting separation from main GC group rather than late-stage accelerations. Most effective when already positioned in front group.
team_tactics: Limited evidence of dedicated team setup; appears to operate more opportunistically from breakaways or when already isolated. No clear pattern of domestique-led tempo control.
terrain_preference: Hard Monument-style finale climbs and technical/cobbled stages where classics skills apply. Thrives on lumpy terrain requiring all-around strength rather than pure climbing.
vulnerability: Mechanical issues at critical moments compromise GC campaigns. Appears to underperform at Tour de France compared to other Grand Tours (Tour disappointment mentioned, Vuelta success followed).
key_patterns: Monument threat on hard finale climbs: 4th Lombardia, consistent presence on selective terrain
Breakaway exploitation over GC group tactics: attacks from front groups, not peloton punches
Unusual classics skills for GC rider: strongest on cobbles, technical stages marked advantage
Tour de France underperformance pattern: explicit 2019 disappointment, breakthrough came at Vuelta instead
form_trajectory: neutral: 2022 Poland cameo showed residual animation but no decisive impact; peak 2019 form not replicated
